transcript_text:
spk_0: Perfect. So, can you just tell me a little bit about yourself and what you're currently doing?
spk_1: Yeah, sure. So, well, I'm a student at the University of Bath.
spk_1: However, this year I'm on placement, so I'm doing my placement in Bristol.
spk_1: I'm a research assistant.
spk_1: I work with children...
spk_1: that's kind of the premise of what my job is.
spk_1: But other than that, I'm just pursuing an undergraduate degree in psychology and...
spk_1: yeah, and yeah, I lived in the UAE prior to moving to the UK for...
spk_1: for university, so...
spk_0: Cool. So, I'm guessing it's the same as me...
spk_0: you're in your third year at Bath Uni?
spk_1: Yeah... third year, exactly!
spk_0: Okay, cool... So, what does a typical week look like for you at the moment?
spk_1: So, with placement during the week, like weekdays, it's just going to my 9-to-5, and then after that, depending on how much energy I have...
spk_1: sometimes I'll spend time with friends, but most days it is just kind of like the routine of going to the gym, coming back home, having a meal, going to sleep...
spk_1: that kind of thing, catching up on anything that needs doing.
spk_1: And then weekends are mainly where I tend to have plans with friends and have things to look forward to.
spk_1: So that's when I'll do, I guess, the more exciting things.
spk_1: But, yeah, that's what it could look like.
spk_0: Yeah... so, on placement, are you currently living by yourself, or do you live with others?
spk_1: I live by myself.
spk_0: Okay, so you dictate all the food decisions for...?
spk_1: Yes... yeah, yeah.
spk_0: So, just around those food decisions...
spk_0: what does the timetable look like for food?
spk_1: In terms of... when I eat?
spk_0: Yeah... when? When do you eat?
spk_0: Does it change on different days?
spk_1: Yeah, sure. So, on days when I'm working, I tend to skip breakfast, so my first meal will be, like, around lunchtime, and then I will eat again after work.
spk_1: I'll have probably, like, a snack-slash, or like a bigger snack, because I'll probably be hungry by then.
spk_1: And then my, like, final big meal of the day will be after I go to the gym or closer to, like, the evening time when it's like I'm back home and I'm not going to, like, leave the house again?
spk_0: Pretty much, yeah. And has this changed to when?
spk_0: Like, on placement this year, obviously 9 to 5 is a lot more structured.
spk_0: Has that changed compared to when you're at uni, or were you still in that structured mindset before?
spk_1: Yeah, I would say maybe it has changed.
spk_1: I'd say because my lifestyle is more structured now.
spk_1: The times that I eat and, like, what I eat and how much I eat is a bit more consistent.
spk_1: Whereas at uni, I think things were kind of changing day to day, and it was like I could wake up and decide what I wanted my routine for that day to be.
spk_1: So in that sense, in uni, like if I had, like, a morning that I could, like, take slower, I'd have breakfast probably.
spk_1: Whereas now, it's like I just...
spk_1: I want to get as much sleep as I can, and then I'll just wake up and leave the house because of, like, work and stuff.
spk_1: So yeah, I would say it has...
spk_1: I would say it has probably changed.
spk_1: Yeah. In the sense where, like, during uni, I could...
spk_1: I didn't think too much about when I was eating.
spk_1: It was more so just whatever was available at the time.
spk_1: Whereas now, my, like, time to eat, I guess, is more restricted.
spk_1: So it's found itself, like, happening in, like, the same times every day.
spk_1: During the... the work week, at least.
spk_1: Weekends, it's a bit different.
spk_1: I'd say, like, this year and last year isn't too varied.
spk_1: So, like, weekends, like, I'll wake up and I'll...
spk_1: I will have breakfast, and I'll have, like, a meal with friends maybe, kind of like similar to what I do at uni.
spk_0: Yeah, definitely understand that.
spk_0: We're going to switch topics slightly, and we're going to go to social...
spk_0: talk about social media.
spk_0: What platforms do you typically use?
spk_1: My most used, I'd say, are Instagram, Snapchat, WhatsApp (if that counts).
spk_1: Yeah, I'd say those are the ones I use most often.
spk_0: Okay. Do you try to stay away from TikTok, or do you go on TikTok as well?
spk_1: I... I don't have TikTok, actually.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: So, would you say what's the most used out of those...
spk_0: and the least?
spk_1: Instagram, and then the least, I guess, WhatsApp...
spk_1: just because I just use it to message family mainly that won't have, like, Snap or Instagram.
spk_0: Yeah... and on a typical day, how many hours do you reckon you spend on social media?
spk_1: Maybe, like... I want to say four to five.
spk_0: Yeah... and when you're on apps like Instagram, do you ever see any food-related content?
spk_1: Yeah, I do like... get food-related content quite a lot.
spk_1: Mainly just like recipes and...
spk_1: and things that I just end up, like, saving to try for later.
spk_0: Yeah, yeah. And when do you...
spk_0: just save the recipes, or do you follow any, like, creators or influencers or food accounts?
spk_1: No, I don't... I don't actually follow.
spk_1: I, I don't follow any, like, celebrities or anything kind of like of that nature.
spk_1: Like, I usually just follow friends because I feel like...
spk_1: yeah, sometimes it gets kind of annoying seeing certain things.
spk_1: And it's more just when I'm, like, looking for something or I want to be on that, like, explore page that I find things like that, and I'll be like, okay, cool, interesting.
spk_1: I can save that. But in general, I try to make my feed more about the social stuff versus food or exercise or anything.
spk_1: Yeah!
spk_0: So, when you see recipes or find recipes, it's usually just on the explore page.
spk_1: Yeah, exactly.
spk_0: Cool. Makes sense. So, now that we've just talked about that, I'll go a bit more back into the food side of things.
spk_0: You've already explained what you do from morning to evening for food.
spk_0: Is this usually cooking, or is it delivery, or what's the split in a week for you?
spk_1: Sure, yeah. So, weekdays it's mainly cooking, sometimes during lunch.
spk_1: Honestly, it depends on the weather.
spk_1: Sometimes, if the weather is nice, I'll want to, like, leave the office or building that I work in, and then I'll get something.
spk_1: But yeah, most of the time it is cooking.
spk_1: I'd say days where I work from home, I have, like, my...
spk_1: this routine of, like, ordering acai.
spk_1: So that's always ordered, like, at least once a week, just on a day that I'm working at home.
spk_1: And then on weekends, I don't tend to cook as much.
spk_1: So that's if I... if I'm not eating out with friends, and if I am home, I will probably order in.
spk_0: So, how often would you say you eat out with friends on weekends?
spk_1: Maybe... like twice.
spk_0: Yeah, yeah. And for the typical meals, when would you order...
spk_0: delivery versus cook?
spk_0: Would it be sort of for dinner, or on a weekend, would you maybe do it for lunch or breakfast?
spk_1: Yeah, I would say...
spk_1: I'd say it depends on...
spk_1: on what I'm doing that day.
spk_1: Like, I'm trying to think...
spk_1: I don't think I tend to order more in the evening or more at, like, a different time of day necessarily.
spk_1: I think it more so just depends on the plan.
spk_1: So, like, if there's a day where I'm having, like, lunch with a friend and then I come back home and I'm at home for the evening, and then later into the night I get hungry, I'll order then.
spk_1: But vice versa, it could be that I meet a friend for dinner and decide to order breakfast in that morning, for example.
spk_1: On a weekend?
spk_0: Yeah, definitely. So, you'd say you cook for the majority of your meals, but you'll very happily either order or go out for food if the sort of occasion arises and you feel like it...
spk_1: Yeah, definitely...
spk_1: yeah.
spk_0: Is that usually... socially driven?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Cool. So then, for cooking...
spk_0: what does cooking mean for you?
spk_1: So it means... So this year, I've kind of...
spk_1: During uni, I would kind of meal prep a bit more, whereas this year, I've kind of...
spk_1: Because I have most evenings to myself, I found that it's, like, nicer to just decide what I want to do that day.
spk_1: So cooking will be, like, starting from me going to actually get the things and kind of doing, like, a mini grocery shop for that evening or the next evening, and then coming back home, preparing that food, making that meal, and then eating that.
spk_1: And then, if I do have anything left over, that's probably what I'll have for lunch the next day at work, and then...
spk_1: But yeah, that's...
spk_1: That's pretty much what I do.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Yeah... so, you mentioned groceries as well.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Do you sort of top-up shops depending on what you want to cook that day, or would you sort of plan what you're going to have for the week?
spk_1: Yeah, so I've been doing what you mentioned, like initially this year.
spk_1: So I'll just pick what I want for...
spk_1: for that day. Usually, I won't get food that will last me more than, like, two days at a time.
spk_1: That's what I've, like, tended to be doing.
spk_1: And then I'll have that.
spk_1: And then, like you said, if anything needs topping up, then I'll just...
spk_1: I'll just go from there.
spk_0: Yeah... okay, cool.
spk_0: And would you say, when you're cooking, do any meals feel more stressful than others, or more enjoyable?
spk_1: Yeah, usually I tend to, like, stray away from anything that's gonna stress me out, especially after, like, working and stuff.
spk_1: Like, I just kind of want things to be as slow and calm as possible.
spk_1: I mean, there's certain meals...
spk_1: I guess it depends how, like, hungry I am as well.
spk_1: Sometimes I do just want something quick, so I'll just want to put something in the air fryer and, like, something in the oven.
spk_1: So it's, like, cooking at the same time, and I'll just take it out and have that at the same time.
spk_1: Um, but yeah, if I do feel like I have more time—because I do kind of...
spk_1: I tend to enjoy cooking when I'm not in a rush—so if I do feel like I have more time, I guess that's when I can make something that's a bit, like, would be perceived as, like, more stressful.
spk_1: But because I'm not in a rush, the process is a bit more enjoyable, so I'm fine to do it!
spk_0: Yeah. And if you're short on time, you'll still cook over choosing a delivery?
spk_1: Yeah, most times on the weekdays I will, because I find that there are kinds of meals that, honestly, would probably take less time than if I waited for something to, like, arrive.
spk_1: Especially because another thing is...
spk_1: because I live in a building, so I have to collect the food if I've ordered it.
spk_1: And sometimes, like, it's easier for me to just, like, leave something cooking while I, like, shower or tidy up or whatever, and then kind of, like, attend to it versus being stressed out about, like, if I'm doing something whilst the food's arrived and I can't go down and get it.
spk_0: So... yeah, yeah, it makes sense.
spk_0: So, how much do you say you spend in a typical week on food, including your groceries, delivery, going out?
spk_1: I would say about, like...
spk_1: maybe, like, a hundred pounds a week, I would say.
spk_0: Yeah... and does that vary from week to week, or is that pretty consistent?
spk_1: I think, yeah... Sometimes, if I'm visiting friends—like if I go to London to visit friends, for example—we're not really, like, cooking at home, I guess, in that sense.
spk_1: So, like, every meal is kind of out, and then that would mean that I, like, spend closer to 100 or maybe even over a hundred.
spk_1: Yeah, I would say it kind of, like, varies.
spk_1: Also depends... Like I said, if I'm buying lunch at work most weeks or for most of that week, then maybe the expenditure would be a bit higher as well.
spk_1: But if I were to, like, average it out...
spk_1: Yeah, I want to say 100.
spk_0: Okay. And what's the split between food delivery, groceries, and going out in, like, that week?
spk_1: Maybe like £40 at home, £60 out with, like, delivery.
spk_0: Yeah. And I noticed you said earlier that you'd order, like, if you work from home, you order a psy...
spk_0: Are there any typical days that you're more likely to order food in the week?
spk_1: Yeah. So, in terms of working from home, that's one day.
spk_1: So, that usually tends to happen, like, at the start of the week...
spk_1: like on a Monday or Tuesday.
spk_1: And then I would say Fridays as well, because Fridays, if I come back home from work, because it's like the start of the weekend, I won't really feel like cooking necessarily.
spk_1: So, usually, whatever meal I have after work on a Friday will be something that I've ordered in.
spk_1: Unless I'm... I've planned to meet a friend, like, straight after and have that meal out.
spk_1: Either way, it won't be me cooking.
spk_1: And then, yeah, I'd say those, like, days I definitely, like, will probably order something.
spk_1: Saturdays and Sundays...
spk_1: it just varies based on plans.
spk_0: Yeah... so, would you say that's sort of built into your week, so it's more of a habit than a treat?
spk_1: Yeah... at this point...
spk_1: yeah. Side things started off as a treat.
spk_1: Maybe it's just a bit of a habit.
spk_0: Yeah... I totally get that.
spk_0: Makes sense. Same for me as well.
spk_0: So, just talking about your last order: What was the last thing you ordered, and what triggered you to order it?
spk_1: The last thing I ordered...
spk_1: Can I check on my app?
spk_1: Exactly. I'm not good...
spk_1: So, the last thing I ordered was...
spk_1: Five Guys. And that was on a Tuesday.
spk_1: That was on a day that I worked from home.
spk_0: Yeah... classic! Love Five Guys.
spk_0: What was... what was the reason that you ordered it on that day?
spk_0: You're working from home?
spk_1: I think... Oh. So, basically, I've been in Bath for the past few days.
spk_1: So that day was kind of like my last day in Bristol until...
spk_1: like, before coming here.
spk_1: So, I didn't want to, like, buy groceries that I'd then have to, like, leave in the fridge and then, like, or cook something and then not know what to do with it if I had anything left over.
spk_1: So, I decided to just order food in that day.
spk_0: Yeah. So, you plan to spend on food delivery that day?
spk_0: Yeah. Cool. And following that sort of trend...
spk_0: do you usually order from Five Guys, or what would you typically order from a food delivery app?
spk_1: Not necessarily Five Guys.
spk_1: I think sometimes, I guess, like a meal that is like a burger and fries or a burger and a side is kind of what I would go for because I feel like that's what, like, in terms of, like, what's reasonably priced and what will, like, fill me up...
spk_1: I think the... the ratio of those things, it's, like, pretty good.
spk_1: But yeah, I would say the most consistent thing I've been ordering, like we mentioned, is, like, my acai rework from home.
spk_1: But other than that, it varies.
spk_1: But yeah, places that sell, like, burgers...
spk_1: that range of food, sure, that's what I'd order!
spk_0: Yeah. And then for four years, I...
spk_0: And then you said, like, burgers and chips is the usual go-to.
spk_0: Would it be from specific restaurants, or would you explore?
spk_1: No, I tend to not really explore.
spk_1: One place I order from a lot is Honest Burgers, just because it's right under my house as well.
spk_1: So it tends to, like, come pretty quickly.
spk_1: And then... açaí...
spk_1: I always order from Oakberry.
spk_0: Yeah... cool. And have you ever ordered groceries for delivery?
spk_1: Yes, I think I did once.
spk_1: I think I was, like, ill that day.
spk_1: Um, but it wasn't like a full, full...
spk_1: like groceries thing.
spk_1: It was kind of just, like, random things that I needed.
spk_1: Um, but yeah, I have...
spk_1: I ordered it from Sainsbury's.
spk_0: Okay. And was the main driver for that just because you were ill and didn't feel like going out to get groceries, or was there another reason?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah, I was...
spk_1: I was just ill that day.
spk_1: The Sainsbury's is really close to my house, so it's one that I normally just, like, go down and get what I need from.
spk_1: But that day, I just wasn't feeling great.
spk_0: Yeah, yeah... And what stopped you from doing it more than just that one time you were ill?
spk_1: Honestly, I kind of enjoy going, like, just to...
spk_1: so that I'm not stuck at home all day.
spk_1: It's just kind of nice to, like, still go down and, like, actually, like, get my stuff and, like, physically see what I'm getting and just feel like I'm, I guess, like, interacting with the world a bit.
spk_1: I tend to also just go after the gym, and the Sainsbury's is on my way back home from the gym, so if I'm going to pick up food for what I'd eat after, I'll just head there because that just makes the most sense!
spk_0: Yeah, totally! And...
spk_0: have you seen any promos for grocery delivery recently?
spk_1: Yeah, I think... I think I have maybe on the app.
spk_1: Sometimes I feel like I've seen, like, there be, like, oh, maybe, like, a certain pound delivery, or it's, like, free delivery or something from a specific, like, place.
spk_1: But yeah, I don't usually tend to order my groceries.
spk_0: Yeah... and what app would that be?
spk_1: This is... I'm talking about delivery.
spk_0: Yeah... so, is delivery the only one, you'd say?
spk_1: Yeah, I think I... I still have UberEats, but I...
spk_1: I normally just use delivery.
spk_0: Yeah. Cool. And a sort of an in-between...
spk_0: Obviously, you've experienced the working world this year, so it's different from being a student.
spk_0: Do you think how you use delivery apps has changed from last year to this year?
spk_1: Yeah, I think so, yeah.
spk_1: So, last year, being in Bath as well, because I lived in town, everything was really close to me.
spk_1: So I think I... I rarely ordered in unless, like, I...
spk_1: I really, like, wasn't feeling like it that day.
spk_1: But most of the time, it was really easy for me to just go down and get some...
spk_1: something in Bristol.
spk_1: Obviously, Bristol is a lot bigger, so if I want something specifically, I'm not necessarily going to go out of my way to, like, walk that long or take a bus just to pick up the food.
spk_1: So I'd say I've been more, like...
spk_1: I've been more inclined to just, like, open my phone and reach for the delivery app than...
spk_1: than last year. Yeah!
spk_0: And is that mainly just based on the convenience aspect of it?
spk_1: Yeah, I would say it's based on the convenience aspect.
spk_1: Also, like, taking into account the fact that, like, at uni, I feel like because you're with friends most of the time, you kind of just do whatever everyone's doing.
spk_1: So whether we're eating out or cooking together or getting groceries together, that kind of happens pretty naturally.
spk_1: Whereas now, because it's, like, work, you get a bit more alone time, I'd say, at least for me.
spk_1: So in that sense, like, because I'm not necessarily, like, looking to others to see, like, what everyone else is doing, I'm more likely, if I'm really not feeling like cooking that day or I'm really not feeling like going out of my way to buy something...
spk_1: I just have that. So, time...
spk_1: convenience, like the social aspect, I guess, as well, that plays a role!
spk_0: Yeah, definitely. And that sort of social...
spk_0: social aspect as well.
spk_0: Did you ever consider, like, doing group ordering when you're at uni?
spk_1: Normally, we just...
spk_1: Yeah. So, if we're gonna do group ordering, we'll order from, like, one person's phone and then kind of, like, transfer.
spk_1: That's... if that's what you mean by group order.
spk_0: Yeah, yeah... So, ordering in a group.
spk_0: But you'd all use one person's account?
spk_0: You wouldn't all, like, choose different things on different people?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah...
spk_1: we've definitely done that.
spk_1: That's what we normally tend to do.
spk_0: Cool, great. And so, just back onto the...
spk_0: the apps that you use.
spk_0: So, you said Deliveries remain, and then you also use UberEats.
spk_0: Do you use any other apps, or...
spk_0: those two are the only ones you use?
spk_1: It's just... those two.
spk_0: And why is your go-to delivery over UberEats?
spk_1: I honestly think it's just because I sometimes forget...
spk_1: like, with Uber, I just use it as, like, if I actually need an Uber.
spk_1: I don't really think to, like, necessarily go on it for food all the time.
spk_1: Also, because I've been using Deliveroo since, like, before uni, that's kind of like what I'm used to just using to order food in general.
spk_1: I don't think we have Uber Eats back home.
spk_1: I... not if I remember.
spk_1: Anyways... yeah. Point is, I...
spk_1: I would just use Deliveroo.
spk_1: So I've... that's just...
spk_1: I naturally just click it here as well.
spk_0: Yeah... so that's just your preference.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: And... have you seen anything from any food delivery apps that stood out to you recently?
spk_1: No... not really.
spk_0: Yeah, totally fine.
spk_0: And when you order in that moment, what's the most important factor?
spk_0: So, a couple examples: you might have price, speed, offers, the subscription benefits, or just the habit...
spk_0: that's what you usually go to.
spk_1: Yeah, I would say...
spk_1: I would say price and the time that, like, the delivery time, are the most important for me.
spk_0: Okay. Yeah, cool. We're just going to do a little exercise now, just rating those factors between the different food delivery apps.
spk_0: Because you've used Deliveroo and Uber Eats, we'll just do it between those two, and we'll rate them between 1 and 5.
spk_0: So, 1 will be extremely bad; 5 will be extremely good.
spk_0: So, we'll do price first.
spk_0: What would you rate Deliveroo and Uber on price from one to five?
spk_1: So... five being good?
spk_0: You said five, being the best.
spk_1: Yeah, I would say...
spk_1: I think... I guess both are four.
spk_1: I wouldn't necessarily say...
spk_1: I've noticed a very big difference in price.
spk_1: Yeah. Might also be just because I don't use Uber Eats as often, so I might not, like, know, but yeah, definitely.
spk_0: What about offers and promotions?
spk_1: I rarely see offers, I think, on Uber Eats or the times that I've been on it.
spk_1: I would say maybe like a three or two with delivery...
spk_1: I might also just say three.
spk_1: I don't think I necessarily, like, notice it as much.
spk_1: I think because when I am ordering, I'll just...
spk_1: I'll... I'll kind of, like, know what I want.
spk_1: I don't think I, like, browse as much.
spk_1: It's kind of more like, oh, I want to...
spk_1: I feel like getting this.
spk_1: I'll just go on it straight away.
spk_1: So maybe I just don't pay attention to it.
spk_0: Yeah, definitely makes sense...
spk_0: What about speed?
spk_1: I'll give... I'll give delivery for the same with Uber Eats.
spk_1: Again.
spk_0: Yeah, cool. And just around thinking about price...
spk_0: because you said they're very similar...
spk_0: if one app had a restaurant you wanted, but another app is cheaper, would you change apps to go to that cheaper restaurant and explore and find that, or would you stay with the app that you're more familiar with?
spk_1: I would probably change apps, or I just use that app to order from that restaurant.
spk_1: The only thing is, sometimes I feel that, like, on one app—not like specifically—but sometimes I feel like maybe on one app, the...
spk_1: the delivery price, for example, will be different.
spk_1: So it might be, like, free on one app and...
spk_1: and cost a bit more on another, but then the...
spk_1: the order itself will end up costing, like, a pretty similar amount, even if you think it's, like, cheaper on...
spk_1: on one versus the other.
spk_1: At least that's my impression.
spk_1: So... so, yeah, if I...
spk_1: if I notice, like, there's a significantly, like, cheaper option on another app, then I will just use that.
spk_1: Yeah!
spk_0: When you say 'significantly cheaper,' how much cheaper would it have to be for you to change apps?
spk_1: So, yeah, maybe, like...
spk_1: if it's, like, £4 cheaper.
spk_0: Okay, yeah, interesting...
spk_0: Now we're just going to move on.
spk_0: Talk about subscriptions between the different apps: Are you currently subscribed to any delivery apps?
spk_0: Okay. And have you ever subscribed in the past?
spk_1: No.
spk_0: And I... So, what's the reason for this?
spk_0: Are you aware of different subscriptions?
spk_1: Yeah, I think it's more...
spk_1: I, I don't know if I've...
